verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal protein that binds to the sperm surface post-translationally, becoming stably associated with the sperm plasma membrane (postacrosomal/equatorial segment) in a wash-resistant manner relevant to sperm-egg fusion. It lacks a transmembrane domain of its own, but its stable surface association on sperm is well-documented. This represents both tissue-restricted (germline/reproductive lineage) surface display and stable surface attachment to a cell-type-specific membrane compartment, qualifying as contextual rather than yes due to the narrow germline/sperm-restricted context.
